title: Interpret Real-Time Item Exchange KPIs on the System Metrics Page
summary: Use the System Metrics Aveva API page to view and interpret destination-level item exchange KPI fields, lane identifiers, status values, and zone information shown in real time.
when_to_use: Use when an operator needs to read the System Metrics Aveva API page and identify the currently displayed item exchange KPI values, lane name, lane system, status, and zone information for a destination or lane.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to infer corrective actions from KPI values.
Do not use this runbook to troubleshoot missing fields or undocumented status meanings beyond what is shown in this source.
Do not use this runbook to change zone configuration or system settings.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: View the System Metrics page and read the displayed KPI, lane, status, and zone values exactly as shown.

safety_notes: This source describes a screen interpretation task only and does not include physical intervention or control actions.
Record or communicate values exactly as displayed without adding unsupported interpretation.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the System Metrics Aveva API page
Visual access to the KPI screen or training reference image

access_required: Access to the System Metrics Aveva API page
system_preconditions: The System Metrics Aveva API page is available for viewing.

visual_references.description: Training video frame showing the System Metrics Aveva API page for real-time KPI tracking of item exchanges by destination or lane.

steps.title: Open or view the System Metrics page
steps.purpose: Access the source screen used to view real-time item exchange KPI information.
steps.instruction: Open or view the System Metrics Aveva API page used for real-time tracking of KPIs for item exchanges.
steps.expected_result: The System Metrics Aveva API page is visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The page is visible and shows the KPI view for item exchanges.
steps.failure_condition: The System Metrics page is not visible or the required KPI view cannot be accessed.

actions.label: View System Metrics page
actions.exact_action: Open or view the System Metrics Aveva API page used for real-time tracking of KPIs for item exchanges.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System Metrics Aveva API page showing destination-level item exchange KPI fields.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the required KPI page is not visible.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: These are real time relatively KPIs...

steps.title: Identify destination-level KPI fields
steps.purpose: Confirm which KPI fields are presented on the page for the viewed destination.
steps.instruction: Identify the destination-level KPI fields shown on the page, including Average Item Count Per Exchange, Median System Generated Exchange(s), Last System Generated Exchange, Number of Exchanges, Number of HotBins, Number of MissSorts, Number of SurePost, and Percent of Exchanges.
steps.expected_result: The displayed KPI fields are recognized and can be read from the page.
steps.healthy_condition: The listed KPI fields are visible and readable on the page.
steps.failure_condition: One or more required KPI fields are not visible or cannot be identified from the page.

actions.label: Read KPI fields
actions.exact_action: Identify the destination-level KPI fields shown on the page, including Average Item Count Per Exchange, Median System Generated Exchange(s), Last System Generated Exchange, Number of Exchanges, Number of HotBins, Number of MissSorts, Number of SurePost, and Percent of Exchanges.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The KPI field list for per-destination item exchange metrics.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if a required KPI field is not visible on the page.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Per destination will give you average item count per exchange, median system generated exchange in seconds, lane name, lane system, last system generated exchange, number of hot bins, number of exchanges, number of miss sorts, number of sure posts, percent of exchanges.

steps.title: Locate lane-identifying information
steps.purpose: Identify which lane the displayed KPI values belong to.
steps.instruction: Locate the lane-identifying information shown on the page, including lane name and lane system.
steps.expected_result: The lane name and lane system are identified for the displayed KPI row or destination.
steps.healthy_condition: Lane name and lane system are visible and readable.
steps.failure_condition: Lane name or lane system is missing or cannot be read.

actions.label: Read lane identifiers
actions.exact_action: Locate the lane-identifying information shown on the page, including lane name and lane system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Lane name and lane system fields on the System Metrics page.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if lane-identifying information is not visible on the page.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Per destination will give you ... lane name, lane system ...

steps.title: Check the status field
steps.purpose: Interpret the displayed status using only the examples provided by the source.
steps.instruction: Check the Status field and compare the displayed value to the source-provided examples such as active, bypassed, or not tracking.
steps.expected_result: The displayed status value is identified and compared against the documented examples.
steps.healthy_condition: The status field is visible and shows a value that can be read directly from the page.
steps.failure_condition: The status field is missing, unreadable, or shows a value whose meaning is not documented in this source.

actions.label: Read status value
actions.exact_action: Check the Status field and compare the displayed value to the source-provided examples such as active, bypassed, or not tracking.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Status field showing example values such as active, bypassed, or not tracking.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the status value is not visible on the page.
Escalate if the displayed status does not match the source-provided examples and no documented meaning is available in this source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The status on which you know it's active, bypassed or not tracking.

steps.title: Locate the zone field
steps.purpose: Identify the zone associated with the displayed side or destination.
steps.instruction: Locate the Zone field and note which zone is shown for the displayed side or destination; the source indicates that each side can have multiple zones.
steps.expected_result: The displayed zone information is identified from the page.
steps.healthy_condition: A zone value is visible and can be read directly from the page.
steps.failure_condition: Zone information is not visible or cannot be identified.

actions.label: Read zone information
actions.exact_action: Locate the Zone field and note which zone is shown for the displayed side or destination; the source indicates that each side can have multiple zones.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Zone field associated with the displayed side or destination.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if zone information is not visible on the page.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: And then what zone... every side has multiple zones... here we have 4.

steps.title: Record or communicate displayed values exactly
steps.purpose: Ensure the observed KPI and identification data are passed on without unsupported interpretation.
steps.instruction: Record or communicate the observed KPI values, status, lane information, and zone exactly as displayed without adding unsupported interpretation.
steps.expected_result: Observed values are documented or communicated exactly as shown on the page.
steps.healthy_condition: The communicated or recorded values match the displayed page content.
steps.failure_condition: Values are missing, altered, or interpreted beyond what the source supports.

actions.label: Document displayed values
actions.exact_action: Record or communicate the observed KPI values, status, lane information, and zone exactly as displayed without adding unsupported interpretation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if required KPI, status, lane, or zone information cannot be read from the page.
Do not infer corrective actions from KPI values because this source only describes what information is displayed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: These are real time relatively KPIs... per destination will give you ... status ... and then what zone.

success_criteria: The System Metrics Aveva API page is visible.
The user can identify the displayed destination-level KPI fields.
The user can read lane name and lane system.
The user can identify the displayed status value.
The user can identify the displayed zone information.
Observed values are recorded or communicated exactly as displayed.
healthy_conditions: The System Metrics page is available for viewing.
KPI fields are visible and readable.
Lane name and lane system are visible.
Zone information is visible and readable.
failure_conditions: The System Metrics page is not visible.
Required KPI fields are missing from the page.
Lane-identifying information is missing or unreadable.
Status is missing, unreadable, or undocumented in this source.
Zone information is missing or unreadable.
Observed values cannot be recorded exactly as displayed.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the required KPI field, status value, or zone information is not visible on the page.
Escalate if the displayed status does not match the source-provided examples and no documented meaning is available in this source.
Do not infer corrective actions from KPI values because this source only describes what information is displayed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System Metrics Aveva API page for real-time item exchange KPI tracking.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: These are real time relatively KPIs... per destination will give you average item count per exchange, median system generated exchange in seconds, lane name, lane system, last system generated exchange, number of hot bins, number of exchanges, number of miss sorts, number of sure posts, percent of exchanges, the status on which you know it's active, bypassed or not tracking. And then what zone...

missing_details: The source does not provide navigation steps for reaching the System Metrics page.
The source does not define thresholds, acceptable KPI ranges, or corrective actions.
The source provides example status values but does not define meanings beyond active, bypassed, or not tracking.
The source does not specify how values should be logged or communicated.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this reference task.
